mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This is the official repository for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for Cluster API to function properly, as they define the custom resources used by the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the CRDs required for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components into the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> Ensure you have the correct namespace and version.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to manage your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API components running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will result in data loss for any clusters managed by Cluster API.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command removes the Cluster API installation.
> This command removes the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster configurations. This step will upgrade the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o yaml > cluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup your current cluster configurations.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD has not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: unable to create infrastructure cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the infrastructure provider is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your infrastructure provider is configured and accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in kube-system are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates a misconfiguration in the Cluster API components.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster API controller is unable to communicate with the Kubernetes API server.

**Fix:**
Check your network settings and ensure that the API server is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version for Cluster resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to migrate your Cluster resources to the new API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your clusters with their statuses.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
